title: Binary Search Trees
analogy: A BST is like a library's card catalog sorted alphabetically — to find a book, you check the current card: if your book comes before it alphabetically, go left; otherwise go right.

overview.summary: A BST is a binary tree where every left descendant is smaller and every right descendant is larger, enabling O(log N) search, insert, and delete on average.
learningObjectives: Understand the BST property and its implications.
Implement search, insert, and delete operations.
Know when BSTs degenerate to O(N) and why balanced BSTs exist.
overview.interviewRelevance: BST operations, validation, and in-order traversal are common interview questions.

paragraphs: Database indexes often use balanced BSTs (Red-Black Trees, B-Trees) to enable fast lookups on sorted data.
C++ std::set and Java TreeSet are implemented as balanced BSTs.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: In-order traversal of a BST produces a sorted sequence — the tree IS a sorted structure.

definition.technicalDefinition: A Binary Search Tree is a binary tree where for every node, all values in the left subtree are strictly less and all values in the right subtree are strictly greater.

vocabulary.definition: Left < Node < Right for every node in the tree.
Left → Node → Right — produces sorted output for a BST.
A BST with O(log N) height guarantee (AVL, Red-Black).

keyProperties: Average case: O(log N) search, insert, delete.
Worst case (skewed): O(N) — degenerates to a linked list.

operations.name: Search
operations.description: Compare target with current node, go left if smaller, right if larger.
operations.visualNote: Path from root lights up as we descend left or right based on comparison.

operations.name: Insert
operations.description: Navigate to the correct leaf position and add the new node.
operations.visualNote: New node appears at the correct leaf position with a spring animation.

practice.title: Validate Binary Search Tree
practice.keyInsight: Pass min/max bounds downward or use in-order traversal.

practice.title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
practice.keyInsight: In-order traversal gives sorted order; return the Kth element.

practice.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST
practice.keyInsight: If both values < node, go left. If both > node, go right. Else, current node is LCA.

practice.title: Delete Node in a BST
practice.keyInsight: Three cases: leaf, one child, or two children (replace with in-order successor).

practice.title: Convert Sorted Array to BST
practice.keyInsight: Pick middle element as root, recurse on left and right halves.

conceptQuiz.question: What does in-order traversal of a BST produce?
conceptQuiz.explanation: In-order visits left-node-right, which for a BST means ascending order.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a binary tree, determine if it's a valid BST.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Validating the BST property (left < node < right for all nodes) is a classic BST problem.
